text.content:
Sandbox layer for Hardby. The party is in the city for Phase 3. Investigation work is densest here; the days when 2-3 PCs are tied up with Veska or Tarsh or the courier-chain leave the rest with time to fill. Hardby is faster than Loftwick, more openly mercantile, and noticeably less protective of strangers — the sandbox locations and NPCs reflect that.

What's in this journal

  • Per-PC anchors — Hardby contacts and locations for each PC.
  • Civic calendar — Hardby week-and-year rhythm, including Gynarchy procedural days and the harbor's tidal calendar.
  • Carousing & information-gathering — d10 tables for what a PC might pick up at a dock-tavern, the Greater Market, the Temple Precinct, or Castrian Vell's salon.
  • Crafting, training, recuperation — what Hardby offers, who provides it, and how Hardby's mercantile pricing differs from Loftwick's.
  • Sub-team mini-quests — five short evening scenes, none touching the active investigation.

How Hardby differs from Loftwick

Three things to keep in mind when running this sandbox layer:

  • Hardby does not protect strangers. The Yeomanry's civic instinct is to make a stranger welcome until proven otherwise. Hardby's instinct is to make a stranger pay until proven otherwise. Anchor NPCs in Hardby are friendly, but the city around them is transactional. PCs who cause trouble find the watch responsive but unsympathetic.
  • The Gynarchy frames everything. Hardby is governed by a council of women drawn from the leading commercial and noble houses; men can hold property and run businesses but cannot sit in the Gynarchy or hold senior civic office. Anchor NPCs include a mix of women and men, but every senior official the party meets — a watch captain, a registrar, a harbor authority — is a woman. PCs accustomed to Yeomanry's mixed civic structure will notice.
  • The conspiracy is operational here. Phase 3's Pressure Clock advances aggressively. Time spent on sandbox activities is real time on the clock. Brief the table: every evening at a salon or a sparring yard is an evening the conspiracy has to act. The mini-quests below are written to resolve in a single evening so the cost is low; longer engagements should be the GM's call.

Cam — the halfling rogue

Anchor location: Hesp's Borrow-and-Sell, a small pawn-and-fence operation in the Salters' Quarter, two streets back from the docks. A narrow shop with a heavy iron gate that locks at the seventh bell.

Anchor NPC: Hesp Olfair, a one-eyed Hardby fence (human, late forties, lost the eye to a tavern accident in his twenties), distantly related to Olfard the Coopered Wreck proprietor (no contact between them in twenty years; do not confuse). Hesp is honest by his profession's standards — he pays a fair fence-rate and does not inform on his sellers — and has a working acquaintance with Mistress Welk in Loftwick (her note of introduction earns Cam an extra 5% on his first sale).

What he offers: 55-65% fence-rate buy-and-sell. Information on Hardby's discount-houses (he knows which are calling loans, which are accepting Aerdy silver, which are short on staff). A back room with watered ale and the day's news. The names of three fences in Greyhawk City he would trust with a referral.

What he needs (if Cam offers): A piece he sold three years ago — a Hardby silver brooch with a tower-and-three-waves countermark — has resurfaced on the docks in someone else's possession. Hesp does not know how it got there and would like it back, quietly. (Mini-quest 1.)

Elle — the halfling monk

Anchor location: The Temple of Ehlonna's practice yard, in the Temple Precinct of the Old City. (See the Hardby Temple District map.) A small stone-walled garden behind the temple proper, with a soft sand floor and a single cypress tree.

Anchor NPC: Sister Wren, an old Flan monk and lay-keeper of the Ehlonna shrine (human, Flan, late seventies, white-haired, weathered to a knot of muscle and bone). Practices the Old Faith breathing forms — older than Brother Ashan's Open Way and structurally different. Welcomes Elle on sight; halfling practitioners are uncommon in Hardby and Sister Wren has not had a proper sparring partner in three years.

What she offers: Sparring (any morning at first light). Old Faith breathing forms Elle has not encountered before. Use of the temple garden for quiet practice. A small clay cup of bitter-herb tea Sister Wren brews; Elle is the only PC who will drink it without flinching.

What she needs (if Elle offers): A young woman of the Hall of the Open Hand (one of the four Temple Precinct shrines) has begun training in the Old Faith forms without consulting Sister Wren or her own clergy. Sister Wren would like the young woman to choose one practice or the other; the conflict is becoming visible. (Mini-quest 2.)

Kitty — the chthonic tiefling druid

Anchor location: Helka's Stilt-House, a small reed-thatched dwelling on raised pilings in the salt-fens a half-day's walk east of Hardby's east wall (not Saltmarsh — the fen is unnamed, smaller, harder to find). Reachable only by a path Helka's tame heron sometimes shows.

Anchor NPC: Helka Fenn, a wisewoman (human, Suel-Flan-mixed, mid-sixties, weathered, walks with a willow staff). Knows the salt-fen's tides, the small things that live in it, and the older things that occasionally move through. Recognizes Kitty's chthonic ancestry on sight and is privately fascinated; she has met one other chthonic descendant in her life and would like to know what Kitty makes of certain dreams.

What she offers: Lodging on the stilt-house's spare cot (free for Kitty, 1 gp/day for any companion). Tides and weather predictions for the Hardby outer harbor and the salt-fens. The use of a small private hide where Kitty can leave offerings. Conversation about chthonic dreams that Kitty may not have wanted to discuss with anyone but will be glad to discuss with Helka.

What she needs (if Kitty offers): A salt-fen heron has been carrying a small length of Hardby thread on its leg for two weeks. Helka would like the thread cut without harming the bird. The bird trusts Kitty. (Mini-quest 3.)

Gianni — the human ranger

Anchor location: The West Gate watch-yard, a small militia courtyard inside Hardby's western wall, with a covered shooting range and a small barracks office.

Anchor NPC: Captain Tarlin Fels, a Hardby city watch captain (human, Hardby-born, mid-forties, lost half her left ear to a southern-district bandit), runs the west-gate roster. The Gynarchy structure means Fels is one of the few municipally-senior women the party will deal with directly outside the Gynarchy registry. Respects Gianni's woodcraft; Fels has worked the wilder roads and knows what good ranger work looks like.

What she offers: Use of the watch-yard's shooting range. Information on Hardby's outer roads (which trade routes are running, which have lost a courier this month, which gates are seeing unusual traffic). The chance to read the watch's daily incident log — it does not include conspiracy material, but it includes the city's normal business and is itself a source.

What she needs (if Gianni offers): A merchant on the Hardby-Saltmarsh road has lost three pack-mules in a fortnight to a predator the watch cannot identify. Captain Fels has had three different reports — a wolf, a wildcat, a bear — and would like Gianni's eye on the tracks before she sends a militia detail to deal with it. (Mini-quest 4.)

Selvara — the human sorcerer

Anchor location: Mistress Vella Korr's tea-house, a small two-story building in Hardby's New City, three streets back from the Greater Market. Quiet by day, busier in the late afternoon when the Scribe's Tea Society meets.

Anchor NPC: Mistress Vella Korr, a retired Hardby trade-clearing notary (human, Hardby-born, late fifties; not related to Mistress Veska Maelan but professionally acquainted). Hosts a small fellowship of Hardby's quieter scholars and arcane practitioners — the Scribe's Tea Society — that meets weekly. The fellowship includes Vell Marad, a sorcerer who corresponds with Master Ferrick of Loftwick (Selvara may already carry Ferrick's letter to him from Loftwick mini-quest 5).

What she offers: Tea (the New City's best, by reputation). Access to the Scribe's Tea Society's weekly meetings (third Earthday of each month, by invitation; carrying Master Ferrick's letter is sufficient). A small private library on the tea-house's second floor, accessible to Society members.

What she needs (if Selvara offers): A young sorcerer who joined the Society six months ago has stopped attending. Mistress Korr would like Selvara, on her next visit, to call at the young sorcerer's lodgings and confirm that she is well. (Mini-quest 5.)

Alicia — the warlock

Alicia's anchor in Hardby is Veska Maelan, but Veska is an investigation NPC and not a downtime contact. For sandbox-layer purposes, Alicia uses Castrian Vell's salon (already established in Phase 3 as the Commercial Cover party's host) — but as a guest rather than a co-host. Castrian welcomes Alicia warmly and lets her sit in the back of the salon during the third-night gatherings without participating, if that is what she wants. Trina has written to Castrian about Alicia; the courtesy holds.

What Castrian offers (downtime specifically): Quiet attendance at his salons. Use of his small upstairs library when no salon is in session. Conversation, if Alicia wants it; silence, if she does not. He is one of the few people in Hardby who recognizes the warlock signs Alicia carries and does not flinch from them.

What he needs (if Alicia offers): Nothing operational. Castrian asks only that Alicia, if she has time during her visit, consent to one private reading of a poem he has been working on for three months and cannot finish. He values her ear, not her advice.

pages.name: Civic Calendar — What's Happening in Hardby This Week

text.content:
Hardby's rhythm is mercantile and tidal. The Gynarchy sets a procedural calendar; the harbor's tide-tables override it twice a day. The festivals overlap with Greyhawk-standard but the local emphasis differs.

The Hardby year

Same Greyhawk twelve-month calendar and four festivals as Loftwick. Local emphasis differs:

  • Needfest — Hardby observes the festival but does not pause; the docks work through midwinter (the southern coastal trade does not stop). Markets close two days at the festival's heart, not three. Temple of Procan holds the largest local observance — the Tide-Bless, a procession from the Temple Precinct down to the harbor's lighthouse and back.
  • Growfest — quiet locally; Hardby does not have agricultural fields to celebrate the planting of. The civic year resumes its full rhythm.
  • Richfest — Hardby's largest civic festival. Three days of harbor games (boat races, swimming competitions, lighthouse-climbing on the second day for the strong-armed). The Gynarchy hosts a public reception on the third day.
  • Brewfest — major in Hardby because the wool and grain harvests inland funnel through the city's docks for export. The Greater Market is at peak; pickpocket gangs are at peak; Hesp's fence-traffic is at peak.

The Hardby week

DayWhat's openNotes
StardayGreater Market (full); docks at full activity; Gynarchy not in sessionThe week's largest market day. Money-changers' alcove busy from sunrise.
SundayTemples (full); markets (small); docks at half-activityReligious observance day. Temple of Procan holds noon service. Other Temple Precinct shrines follow their own rhythms; Sister Wren (Elle's anchor) holds a small Old Faith circle at the second bell after dawn.
MoondayGynarchy in session; docks at full activity; markets (small)Gynarchy day. The Despotrix and her council sit at the Gynarchy House from the second bell. Civic petitioners present in the morning.
GodsdayGynarchy in session; markets (full); docks at full activitySecond main market day. Gynarchy meets briefly to certify the prior week's votes.
WaterdayMarkets (small); docks at full activity; Gynarchy not in sessionThe Scribe's Tea Society meets at Mistress Korr's (Selvara's anchor) on the third Waterday of each month.
EarthdayMarkets (small); Gynarchy committee day; docks at full activityGuild meetings: cooper's at the third bell, sail-maker's at the fifth, ship-fitter's at the seventh.
FreedayMarkets (medium); Gynarchy not in session; docks at half-activityCastrian Vell's salons run the third Freeday evening of each month. Public taverns at peak from the seventh bell to the second bell of the following morning.

The harbor's tidal calendar

Hardby's harbor has two tides per day. High tide governs ship arrivals and departures; low tide governs the dock-workers' meal breaks and (informally) the rhythm of the Greater Market's fish-and-grain trade. The party will notice that the city's street rhythm and its harbor rhythm are out of sync by 2-3 hours and shift by 50 minutes a day.

Practical effect: the docks district is at peak activity 2-3 hours before each high tide and at low ebb during the half-hour after each low tide. PCs investigating the docks (the Coopered Wreck, the Black Cog warehouse, Whalemarket Quay) should plan around this. The Coopered Wreck is at its quietest mid-afternoon between tides — best window for an unobserved approach.

Local events the party may run into

  • The Tide-Bless procession (Needfest only). From the Temple Precinct down to the lighthouse. The party may join as observers; it is open to all.
  • The Procan morning service (Sunday at noon, Temple Precinct). Public.
  • The Scribe's Tea Society (third Waterday of each month, Mistress Korr's tea-house). By invitation; Master Ferrick's letter is sufficient.
  • Castrian Vell's salons (third Freeday evening of each month, his Salters' Quarter house). By invitation; established in Phase 3.
  • The Gynarchy public petition hour (Moonday morning, second to fourth bell, the Gynarchy House). Open to citizens and visitors with cause.
  • The harbor games (Richfest only). Three days of boat races, swimming, lighthouse-climbing. Free to enter; small prizes.

An evening at a Hardby dock-tavern (Coopered Wreck excluded)

Cost: 4-6 sp passable; 8 sp comfortable; 4-6 gp memorable. Persuasion or Insight DC 13 (Hardby is harder to read than Loftwick). On success, roll d10.

d10What you heard
1A Yeomanry trader is buying small lots of Hardby silver above the going price. (False rumor; the trader exists but is paying market rate.)
2The Black Cog Lane warehouse next to the abandoned cooperage has been seeing late-night activity for a fortnight. The dock-workers do not approach it. (Investigation hook — but only the abandoned cooperage's neighbor warehouse, not the conspiracy's.)
3A Salters' Quarter inn is undercutting the Blackwell Inn's prices. The undercutter has only been open for three weeks and the staff do not know him.
4A Hardby coastal galley named the Lacquer Wave sails the Saltmarsh-to-Rel-Astra route weekly; her captain Yorra Mel is honest by Hardby standards.
5A merchant just back from Greyhawk City says the Foreign Quarter's Brass Crow counting-house is taking new clients. He is uncertain if this is unusual. (In Phase 3: foreshadowing of the Phase 5 Greyhawk thread.)
6The Gynarchy registry's clerks have been working late three nights a week. The reason is not public.
7A man at the next table is wearing a brass ring with a tower-and-three-waves device. He is drunk. He says the ring was his uncle's. He is twenty-four years old; he is from Saltmarsh.
8The dock-master's office has had two unexplained inventory discrepancies this week. The dock-master is irritable.
9A small fishing-skiff out of the Hardby outer harbor went missing three days ago. Nobody is panicked yet; the weather has been fair.
10The Coopered Wreck's owner Olfard refused service to a Hardby watch sergeant on Earthday. The sergeant left without a word. The neighborhood is curious. (In Phase 3: this is a useful tell about Olfard's wider relationships, but is not itself an investigation hook.)

A morning at the Greater Market

Cost: free, plus 2-5 sp for purchases. Investigation or Persuasion DC 12. On success, roll d10.

d10What you saw or heard
1The fishmongers' row has the year's best smoked herring. 6 sp for an evening's eating.
2The money-changers' alcove is short of Aerdy silver this week. Anyone trying to change Hardby coin to Aerdy will pay 5% above the standard exchange.
3A ragged old man is selling a single Hardby silver brooch for half its value. Cam recognizes the design — it is the brooch Hesp lost three years ago. (Hook for mini-quest 1.)
4The meat-and-grain row is selling Yeomanry-stamped grain bags above the going price. Yeomanry harvest is light this season.
5A small boy with a kite has lost his footing and his kite. A passing halfling stranger catches it and returns it. The boy beams.
6The Blackwell Inn's evening menu is a bowl of Old Faith mussel-stew Sister Wren may have helped flavor. (Old Faith ingredients.) Sister Wren acknowledges Elle if she walks past Sister Wren's seat.
7A Hardby herald reads a Gynarchy bulletin at the central platform. The bulletin includes routine civic notices and one mention of a small civic petition relating to the Hardby outer harbor's tariff schedule. (Routine.)
8The market watch is investigating a small theft. The thief was a child of eleven. (See Loftwick mini-quest 1 for tonal precedent; not connected.)
9A Hardby silversmith is engaging in unusually spirited haggling with a customer over a small commission. The customer is wearing the Aerdy commercial-court advocate's coat. (Halask's local equivalents are in Hardby this week. No conspiracy implication.)
10A heron lands briefly on the central well's coping with a small length of Hardby thread on its leg. (Hook for Kitty's mini-quest 3.)

An afternoon at Castrian Vell's salon

Salons run the third Freeday evening of each month. By invitation; Trina's letter or a returning-guest's word is sufficient. Persuasion or History DC 13 to make the afternoon yield. On success, roll d10.

d10What you learned
1A poet from Rel Astra is in town for the season. His work is good, his finances are not, his stories about the Naelax embassy are interesting.
2The Gynarchy under-clerk Zoria Weis (a Phase 3 NPC) attended a salon two months ago and has not been seen at one since. Castrian shrugs; civic clerks come and go.
3A Hardby sorcerer named Vell Marad will be at the next salon. He has corresponded with Master Ferrick of Loftwick. (Selvara's contact.)
4An Aerdy scholar of paleography is rumored to be considering a move to the Free City of Greyhawk. Castrian thinks the rumor unreliable.
5The harbor commission of Rel Astra has three vacant seats. Three different guests at the salon have heard this rumor; none can name the seats. (In Phase 3: foreshadowing of Phase 4. In other phases: routine commercial gossip.)
6A retired Hardby ship-captain is about to publish a small memoir of the Aerdi coastal trade. It will not name names but will, the captain hopes, illuminate practices.
7The Gynarchy registry has filed an unusually large number of property-transfer petitions this month. Reason unknown.
8A Hardby bookbinder has acquired a folio of pre-Aerdy Suel poetry. The folio is for sale to the right buyer. Castrian knows the bookbinder.
9A man with a Greyhawk-Foreign-Quarter accent has been attending the salon's edges for two months without speaking. Castrian tolerates him. He has done nothing. He is watching.
10Castrian, late in the evening when his guests have thinned, mentions that a friend in the Stark Mounds region west of Loftwick has written him a small note about a wood-correspondent of Trina's. (In Phase 5: Aldea reference dropped early. In Phase 3: foreshadowing.)

Crafting and gear

ServiceProviderCost / TimeNotes
Mundane weapon repairSeveral smiths in the Merchant Quarter5-8 sp / 1 dayHardby pricing runs 10-20% above Loftwick due to mercantile markup.
Custom weapon commissionMaster Wenra Holst, Merchant Quarterfull PHB price + 30% / 5-10 daysHardby's best weaponsmith. Specializes in coastal arming-swords and naval-officer's hangers.
Armor repair / fittingThe harness-makers' row, Salters' Quarter1-2 gp / 2-3 daysSeveral Salters' Quarter shops fit halfling armor without surcharge.
Sail-maker / canvas workThe sail-makers' row, docks districtvaries / 1-3 daysFor PCs commissioning travel-canvas, ship's-cloth packs, etc.
Bow / archery workMaster Vellan, an Aerdy-trained bowyer in the Merchant Quarter2-4 gp / 1-2 daysCaptain Fels (Gianni's anchor) introduces. Vellan is more skilled than Old Tellis in Loftwick but charges accordingly.
Spell-component refresh (common)Mistress Korr's tea-house (Selvara's anchor)1-2 gp + components at cost / 1 dayKorr stocks more variety than Master Ferrick; rare components available in 1-2 weeks.
Spell-component refresh (rare)Vell Marad (sorcerer member of the Scribe's Tea Society)2-4 gp + components at cost / 1-2 weeksMarad has access to Aerdy and Suel rare components; standing in the Society earns a 10% discount.
Druidic focus / herbal preparationsHelka Fenn at her stilt-housefree for Kitty / 1-2 daysHelka does not deal in coin; small offerings are appropriate.
Identifying a magic item (common)Mistress Korr5 gp / 1 dayCommon items reliably; uncommon in 2-3 days; rare requires Vell Marad's involvement.
Identifying an enchantmentVell Marad (by appointment)10-25 gp / 1-3 daysMarad analyzes any item but charges by complexity. Selvara's standing in the Society earns 15% off.
Notarial / paleographic analysisVeska Maelan (Phase 3 only; see investigation journals)variesInvestigation NPC; not a downtime resource. Listed here only so the GM does not double-book Veska's time.

Training

Hardby is the campaign's best training city for languages and trade-related proficiencies; weakest for Yeomanry-specific or Old Faith disciplines.

Skill / ToolTrainerNotes
Old Aerdi (language)Vell Marad or Mistress KorrBoth fluent.
Suloise (language)Vell MaradMarad's specialty; standard training rate.
Hardby trade cipher (writing)Mistress KorrTrainable; mostly used in commercial contexts. Aerdy commercial-court advocates know it.
Old Faith forms / breathing (Druidic equivalent)Sister Wren at the Ehlonna shrineOpen to monks and druids; will not teach a non-practitioner.
Sail-handling / sailor's toolsCaptain Yorra Mel of the Lacquer Wave (when in port; ask at the docks)Standard rate; passage to Saltmarsh and back included for trainees.
Investigation (skill)Veska Maelan (Phase 3 only, by Veska's invitation)Difficult to obtain; Veska accepts a trainee only on long acquaintance.
Insight (skill)Sister Wren or Captain FelsBoth teach; Fels's approach is procedural, Wren's contemplative.
Athletics / AcrobaticsSister Wren at the Ehlonna shrine gardenOpen to monks and physically-disciplined PCs; standard rate.
Stealth / Sleight of HandHesp Olfair (Cam only; Hesp will not teach a non-trusted PC)Cam may teach to others under Hesp's tolerance.

Recuperation

ServiceProviderCost / DayNotes
Modest inn (Blackwell Inn, Brass Lantern)multiple3-6 gp / dayHardby pricing higher than Loftwick. Three days fully restores.
The Ehlonna shrine's pilgrim cellsSister Wren (Elle free; others 1-2 gp donation)1-2 gp / dayBonus: same as Loftwick's Open Way (the recuperator may end two persistent effects rather than one).
Helka's stilt-houseHelka (Kitty free; others by Helka's invitation, 1 gp/day)variesBonus: same as the Reedrush — recuperator wakes with one Inspiration die after the third day. Plus: Helka may offer a small-bird scrying-omen reading on the third morning, GM's discretion.
Mistress Korr's tea-house upper roomsMistress Korr (Selvara free; Society members 2 gp/day)variesQuiet, scholarly. Includes use of the upstairs library.
The Temple of Procan's pilgrim hospiceopen to all, donation 5 sp - 2 gp/dayvariesSpecialty: maritime ailments (sea-fever, salt-shore poisoning, drowning recovery). Standard recuperation otherwise.
Cure disease / restoration (clerical)Temple of Procan, Temple of Ehlonna50-100 gp / 1 dayProcan handles maritime; Ehlonna handles natural / wild disease. Higher-grade illnesses may require Greyhawk City or beyond.

1. Cam — "The Brooch That Came Back" (rogue affinity)

Hesp Olfair sold a Hardby silver brooch with a tower-and-three-waves countermark to a Hardby-side merchant three years ago. The brooch has reappeared on a man at the Greater Market who is selling it for half its value. Hesp would like the brooch back, the man identified, and the chain of resale traced enough to know how it returned to Hardby unobserved.

The man is Saltmarsh-born, twenty-four, and the brooch was his uncle's. The uncle bought it from a Hardby fence (not Hesp) eighteen months ago and gave it to the man's mother (his sister) before he died. The mother passed it to her son three months ago when he came to Hardby looking for work. The man is selling it because he has no work and is hungry.

How it resolves: Cam approaches at the Greater Market. Persuasion DC 13 to learn the chain. Investigation DC 12 to confirm the brooch is the one Hesp sold. The right answer is to buy the brooch back at Hesp's expense (Hesp will reimburse 4 gp; the man asks 3 gp), give the man two silver and Hesp's name as a possible employer (Hesp can use a runner two days a week), and report the chain to Hesp. The wrong answer is to buy cheap and walk; Hesp learns and is disappointed.

Reward: Hesp's standing fee on Cam's next three sales rises to 65%. Hesp's records open: he will share his Hardby fence-network's understanding of the harbor's recent silver-flow on request.

2. Elle — "The Two Schools" (monk affinity)

Sister Wren has noticed a young woman of the Hall of the Open Hand training in the Old Faith forms three mornings a week without consulting Sister Wren or her own clergy. The young woman, Sister Marrin, is twenty-two, a recently-vowed sister of the Hall, and is afraid that if she asks her clergy openly they will forbid her. Sister Wren would like Marrin to choose one practice or the other; the conflict is becoming visible in Marrin's posture and breathing.

How it resolves: Elle observes Marrin's morning practice (Insight DC 12 to read the conflict). Elle then meets Marrin and Sister Wren together at the Ehlonna shrine in the early afternoon — Sister Wren has invited Marrin formally, no other clergy present. The right answer is for Elle to demonstrate that the two schools' breathing forms are not, in fact, in conflict at the level Marrin is practicing them, and that her senior clergy will likely permit a syncretic practice if asked. The wrong answer is to take a side; either practice will lose Marrin one path and the other practice's resentment.

Reward: Sister Wren grants Elle the Old Faith breathing forms in their full sequence (a small training-progress benefit at the GM's discretion — perhaps a one-time +1 to a Constitution save in a future scene). Marrin will, if Elle asks in any future phase, carry a discreet message between the two shrines.

3. Kitty — "The Heron's Thread" (druid affinity)

Helka Fenn has noticed a salt-fen heron carrying a small length of Hardby thread on its leg for two weeks. Helka does not know how the thread came to be there; the heron is one of her tame ones and would not have been caught accidentally. She would like the thread cut without harming the bird and the thread itself given a careful look — Helka has a feeling, not a thought.

The thread is Hardby-spun, dyed dark green with a single small bronze bead at one end. It was tied around the heron's leg by a Hardby New City girl of nine who was trying to send a message to her grandmother in the salt-fens; the grandmother lives an hour's walk from Helka's stilt-house. The girl did not know herons fly long. The grandmother has not seen the heron because she does not look up at herons.

How it resolves: Kitty walks to the salt-fens and meets the heron at first light (Animal Handling DC 11 to retrieve the thread without alarming the bird). Investigation DC 12 to read the thread (Hardby-spun, the bead is a Hardby New City craft, the dye is a New City dye). Kitty walks the thread to the grandmother's cottage, sits with the grandmother for an hour, and learns where the granddaughter lives. Walks the thread back to the New City and gives it to the girl. The right answer is the whole walk. The wrong answer is to keep the thread; the grandmother will worry; the heron will continue to be patient about strangers.

Reward: Helka teaches Kitty a single Old Faith small-rite for asking a bird to carry a message faithfully (one-shot, GM's discretion; Kitty may use it once per phase).

4. Gianni — "The Three Reports" (ranger affinity)

Captain Tarlin Fels has had three different reports of a predator on the Hardby-Saltmarsh road: a wolf, a wildcat, a bear. Three pack-mules lost in a fortnight. Captain Fels would like Gianni's eye on the tracks before she sends a militia detail.

The predator is a single old worg (CR 1/2 SRD entry) that drifted south from the Stark Mounds two months ago and has been picking off mules at night because they are easy prey. It is not aggressive toward humans and has not yet developed a taste for them. The three different witness reports came from three different witnesses' panics; nobody saw the worg cleanly.

How it resolves: Gianni rides the road with one of Fels's deputies (a militia trooper named Tessen, twenty-eight). Survival DC 12 to identify the worg's track from the three confused witness statements. The right answer is to track the worg back to its day-lair (a small cave near a road-side spring), brief Captain Fels privately, and let Fels arrange a humane removal — most likely a poisoned mule-carcass at the lair's mouth. The wrong answer is to engage the worg directly; it is old but still dangerous to a single ranger, and the encounter is not the point.

Reward: Fels's deputy Tessen will, in any future phase, respond to Gianni's call faster than to anyone else's. Fels grants Gianni a written authorization to enter any Hardby city watch-yard at any hour with no questions.

5. Selvara — "The Sorcerer Who Stopped Coming" (sorcerer affinity)

Mistress Korr would like Selvara, on her next visit, to call at the lodgings of Sianna Reff, a young Hardby sorcerer who joined the Scribe's Tea Society six months ago and has stopped attending. Sianna is twenty-three, lives in two rooms over a glover's shop in the Merchant Quarter, and has not been seen in public for three weeks.

Sianna is alive and unharmed. She has stopped attending the Society because her sorcerous awakening — which the Society had been helping her manage — has begun manifesting in ways that frighten her, and she is not yet ready to discuss them. Specifically, she has begun to dream other people's dreams. The dreams are not malicious or dangerous; they are exhausting and lonely, and Sianna does not know how to stop.

How it resolves: Selvara calls at Sianna's lodgings. Insight or Persuasion DC 13 to read what Sianna is actually struggling with (she will not say it openly). The right answer is to recognize the dream-borrowing as a known sorcerous manifestation, share Selvara's own experience with adjacent phenomena (or her honest acknowledgment that she has not faced this specifically), and gently bring Sianna back to the Society — to Mistress Korr first, alone, before any group meeting. The wrong answer is to dismiss the dreams as "just a sorcerer thing"; Sianna will retreat further.

Reward: Mistress Korr extends Selvara's standing in the Scribe's Tea Society to lifetime. Sianna, in any future phase, will be a contact in Hardby's quieter sorcerous corners.
